title: BlackSky Wins Government Contract for Gen-2 Satellite Services
text: BlackSky Technology Inc. (NYSE:BKSY) has secured a seven-figure subscription contract with a government customer for its Gen-2 satellite constellation services, providing access to high-resolution imagery and AI-enabled analytics capabilities. Contract Details: The one-year agreement grants the customer access to BlackSky's satellite capacity for mission-critical applications. CEO Brian O'Toole remarked that Gen-2 constellation continues to provide tremendous real-world operational benefits to their global clientele. Company Performance: The announcement comes as BlackSky's stock has experienced significant momentum, surging 259% over the past year and currently trading at $41.38 with a $1.54 billion market capitalization. The company maintains impressive gross profit margins of 69% according to recent financial metrics. Financial Outlook: BlackSky reported Q1 2026 revenue of $20.8 million. The firm has projected full-year 2026 revenue between $130 million and $150 million, representing growth of 22% to 41%. Jefferies elevated its price target to $50.00 from $27.00, maintaining a Buy rating based on backlog expansion confidence. Operations: The Virginia-based company operates low-Earth orbit satellites combined with its Spectra analytics software platform, delivering subscription-based services to government agencies and commercial clients globally.
